input.summary: Assign F2: C++ market core + parity gate
input.message:
Next task: F2 — the C++ market core, parity-proven against the Python prototype. This is the most important correctness gate of the whole project. Same rules: work in /workspace/jnx-feed, NO git commands. You own cpp/Makefile edits (another agent is working in cpp/wire/ and cpp/test/test_record.cpp and will NOT touch the Makefile — ignore any cpp/wire files you see appear, and do NOT add them to the Makefile).

FIRST read, in this order:
1. /workspace/jnx-feed/JNX_PLAN2.md — Phase F2 (all subsections) + §2 (table schema, for field naming).
2. /workspace/jnx-feed/JNX_PLAN.md §3.3 "Semantics gotchas" — every one of the 6 rules must be honored.
3. THE PROTOTYPE CODE — this is the reference implementation you are porting; read it thoroughly BEFORE writing C++: jnxfeed/book/refdata.py, jnxfeed/book/orderbook.py, jnxfeed/book/tape.py, jnxfeed/book/market.py, and jnxfeed/types.py. Match its semantics EXACTLY, including counter semantics (orphans, collisions, auto-created books) and any subtle choices (e.g. when exactly an order is removed, how U orphans are handled, how trades are recorded). Where the prototype and the plan text disagree, THE PROTOTYPE WINS (note the discrepancy in your final message).

Deliverables:
1. tools/proto_state_dump.py — Python (runs on python3 = 3.14; imports jnxfeed with repo root on sys.path). Usage: python3 tools/proto_state_dump.py <file.itch> <outdir>. Replays every message through the prototype Market and writes the canonical dump: refdata.csv, books.csv (every ticker+group: ALL price levels, bids best-first then asks best-first, each level = price,qty,order_count; plus per-book totals), orders.csv (every live order, sorted by order_number), trades.csv (per ticker+group: cum_qty, cum_turnover, trade_count, last_price, last_qty, last_match_number), stats.csv (orphan counts by type, collision count, auto-created book count, messages applied). RULES for canonical form: header row in each file; rows sorted deterministically (ticker, then group, then price/order_number); ALL prices/quantities as raw integers (no floats, no division); LF line endings; no timestamps or absolute paths in output. Document the exact column list in a comment block at the top — the C++ dumper mirrors it.
2. cpp/market/refdata.h/.cpp — consumes R/L/H/Y/S + ref-price A (order_number==0); absence-semantics defaults; auto-create unknown books with directory_seen=false; tick tables (map id -> sorted (price_start, tick_size) rows).
3. cpp/market/orders.h/.cpp + cpp/market/levels.h/.cpp — std::unordered_map<uint64_t,Order> store; per-(ticker,group) books with std::map<uint32_t,Level> asks and std::map<uint32_t,Level,std::greater<uint32_t>> bids; A/F insert; E cumulative execution, trade price = STORED PASSIVE ORDER price, erase order at exactly zero remaining; D erase; U = erase original + insert new inheriting ticker/group/side from ORIGINAL, price/qty from message, orphan-U inserts NOTHING; orphan E/D/U counted per type, never fatal; E with executed_qty > remaining clamps to zero with a warning counter; order-number collision counter.
4. cpp/market/tape.h/.cpp — per-(ticker,group) cum_qty, cum_turnover (sum price*qty as uint64), trade_count, last trade fields.
5. cpp/market/market.h/.cpp — Market::apply(const ItchMsg&) -> ApplyResult routing to the above; ApplyResult carries: bool applied, affected ticker+group (if any), which sections changed (static/state/book/trade bitmask), and the order-delta (op char A/E/D/U, order_number, orig_order_number, side, price, qty, order_type) — this feeds record building in a later phase. Also accessors the dumper needs (iterate books/orders/refdata/tape) and the counters.
6. cpp/tools/book_dump.cpp -> cpp/build/book_dump: same CLI as proto_state_dump.py, writes byte-identical files.
7. cpp/test/test_market.cpp — unit tests for every §3.3 gotcha: ref-price A never enters the book (incl. NO_PRICE); E passive-price trade + multi-fill (several E on one order, one trade each, removal at zero); over-execution clamp; D unknown; U inherit + U orphan; auto-create semantics; absence defaults for H/Y; a randomized invariant test (generate a few thousand random A/E/D/U with a seeded RNG: after each message, per-book level totals == sum of live orders in the store for a sampled book; end with full check over all books).
8. Makefile: add market module + book_dump binary (do NOT add cpp/wire).

VERIFICATION (all must pass; run and paste outputs):
  make -C cpp clean && make -C cpp test && make -C cpp all
  python3 tools/proto_state_dump.py tests/fixtures/sample_udp_head.itch /tmp/f2_py_head
  cpp/build/book_dump           tests/fixtures/sample_udp_head.itch /tmp/f2_cpp_head
  diff -r /tmp/f2_py_head /tmp/f2_cpp_head          # MUST be empty
  python3 tools/proto_state_dump.py tests/fixtures/sample_udp.itch /tmp/f2_py
  cpp/build/book_dump           tests/fixtures/sample_udp.itch /tmp/f2_cpp
  diff -r /tmp/f2_py /tmp/f2_cpp                    # MUST be empty — bit-identical on the FULL golden sample
  python3 -m pytest -q                              # prototype suite still 203 passed
Iterate on the head fixture first (fast loop), full sample last. If diffs appear, the C++ is wrong (or your Python dumper is non-deterministic) — fix the code, NEVER relax the diff or post-process files.

HARD RULES: C++11 only, -Werror -pedantic clean, zero external deps, no git. Canonical dumps must never rely on unordered_map iteration order — sort explicitly. Final message: files created/changed, both diff results, key stats.csv numbers (orphans/collisions/auto-created on the full sample), test tail, and any prototype-vs-plan discrepancies you found.

input.summary: Assign F3: wire format spec and codecs
input.message:
Next task: F3 — the wire/record format: frozen byte-layout spec, C++ codec, Python decoder, cross-language golden vectors. Same rules as before: work in /workspace/jnx-feed, NO git commands, don't modify existing files except as stated.

CRITICAL COORDINATION RULE: another agent is working concurrently in cpp/market/, cpp/tools/, tools/proto_state_dump.py and OWNS cpp/Makefile. You must NOT edit cpp/Makefile and must NOT run `make -C cpp` (racy build dir). Compile your C++ with explicit g++ commands into /tmp/f3_build/ (create it). The orchestrator wires your module into the Makefile afterwards. Do not touch anything under cpp/market or cpp/itch/soup sources (you may #include and link their .cpp files in your manual builds — they are committed and stable).

FIRST read:
- /workspace/jnx-feed/JNX_PLAN2.md — §3 "Wire format" (the record definition — authoritative), §2 (table fields the record carries), Phase F3, and §0 ground rules.
- cpp/common/endian.h, cpp/test/jsonvec.h (existing style), cpp/itch/itch.h (types/naming conventions).
- JNX_PLAN.md §0 (Python 3.6 rules) — jnxweb/records.py must obey them and pass tools/py36check.py.

Deliverables:
1. docs/wire_spec.md — THE contract. For the 8-byte header and EVERY record kind (U update, O order, K tick, B sync_begin, E sync_end, G get_state, H hello, R reset): a markdown table with columns offset | field | size | type | notes, exact total sizes, big-endian everywhere, alpha = ASCII space-padded. Follow plan §3's field list and order exactly (epoch, pub_seq, session, exch_seq, exch_ns, trigger, ticker, group; static section incl. a static/flags byte; state section; book section: level_count bid u8 + level_count ask u8 + 10 bid levels + 10 ask levels of (price u32, qty u32, order_count u32) + total_bid_qty u64 + total_ask_qty u64 + total_bid_orders u32 + total_ask_orders u32; trade summary section; delta section). Where plan §3 is loose (exact flag bit numbering, unused-field fill), YOU decide, document it in the spec, and both codecs follow it. State the frozen total UPDATE size prominently.
2. cpp/wire/record.h + record.cpp — namespace jnx. Host-order POD structs: UpdateRecord (flat struct with all sections), OrderRecord, TickRecord, HelloRecord, SyncEndRecord (session char[11], last_exch_seq, epoch); encode functions writing header+body via endian.h helpers returning total bytes; decode functions (validate magic 0x4A58, version 1, kind, body_len == expected for kind; reject otherwise with reason). RecordFramer: feed(bytes)/next(RawRecord&) incremental stream framer (same pattern as SoupFramer — read cpp/soup/soup.h for the interface style, but do not modify it). Zero-fill ALL unused/padding bytes on encode — no uninitialized memory on the wire. Include a compile-time or startup check pinning the encoded UPDATE size to the spec's frozen number.
3. cpp/test/test_record.cpp (minitest.h style) — round-trip every record kind incl. edge cases: empty book (level_counts 0, zero-filled levels), full 10+10 levels, NO_PRICE ref price, delta ops A/E/D/U/'#', all-flag combinations; framer fed byte-at-a-time and with split header; corrupted magic/version/len rejected; UPDATE encodes to exactly the frozen size with two different payloads (byte-compare unused regions are zero).
4. cpp/tools/gen_record_vectors.cpp — small tool: writes cpp/test/vectors/records.bin = concatenated encoded records, one of each kind plus the edge cases above (deterministic fixed values, no clock/random), and prints a manifest of what it wrote.
5. jnxweb/ package start: jnxweb/__init__.py (empty ok) + jnxweb/records.py — Python 3.6-safe decode of the header and ALL record kinds (UPDATE fully: return a dict or NamedTuple with the same field names as the C++ struct), using struct with explicit '>' formats per the spec; also a decode_stream(bytes)->list helper for framed streams. Must import and run on python3 (3.14) AND pass py36check.
6. tests/unit/test_records_py.py — pytest: runs the gen tool's output (check records.bin into the repo after generating) through jnxweb.records; asserts every field of every record matches the known values the C++ generator wrote (hardcode the expected values in the test, mirroring the generator); asserts UPDATE size constant matches the spec number quoted from docs/wire_spec.md.

VERIFICATION (run all, paste outputs):
  mkdir -p /tmp/f3_build && g++ -std=c++11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic -O2 -g -pthread -I cpp -o /tmp/f3_build/test_record cpp/test/test_record.cpp cpp/wire/record.cpp && /tmp/f3_build/test_record
  g++ (same flags) -o /tmp/f3_build/gen_record_vectors cpp/tools/gen_record_vectors.cpp cpp/wire/record.cpp && /tmp/f3_build/gen_record_vectors cpp/test/vectors/records.bin
  python3 -m pytest tests/unit/test_records_py.py -q
  python3 tools/py36check.py jnxweb/__init__.py jnxweb/records.py
  python3 -m pytest -q     # whole suite still green
Also re-run gen_record_vectors twice and cmp the outputs (determinism).

REMEMBER: after this task the layout is FROZEN — get the spec right and make the doc unambiguous enough that a different model could reimplement either codec from it alone. Final message: files created, the frozen UPDATE total size, spec decisions you made where the plan was loose, verification outputs.

Both agents are dispatched in parallel:

- **Agent A** (reused, has the codec context) — **F2, the critical gate**: port of the prototype's book/refdata/tape logic to `cpp/market/`, plus the two canonical state dumpers (`tools/proto_state_dump.py` and `cpp/build/book_dump`). Its hard acceptance is an **empty `diff -r`** between the Python-prototype dump and the C++ dump over the full 222k-message golden sample — with explicit instructions that the prototype code wins over the plan text on any semantic disagreement, and that relaxing the diff is forbidden.
- **Agent B** (reused, knows the prototype and record conventions) — **F3, the frozen wire format**: `docs/wire_spec.md` byte-layout contract, the C++ record codec + stream framer in `cpp/wire/`, the Python 3.6-safe decoder `jnxweb/records.py`, and cross-language golden vectors (`records.bin` generated by C++, field-asserted from Python).

To keep them from colliding: Agent A owns `cpp/Makefile` and the make build dir; Agent B compiles with standalone g++ commands and I'll wire its module into the Makefile when I verify and commit. The Market-coupled record builder (`make_update`) is deferred to F5 where the FH assembles records, so F3 has no dependency on F2.

I'll verify each independently (full rebuild, parity diffs, py36check, whole test suite) and commit `F2: …` / `F3: …` as they land.

<result>Whole suite green: 214 passed (203 pre-existing + my 11 new). Sanity check done: all six deliverables exist as new files only, cpp/Makefile untouched, no make or git commands run, builds went to /tmp/f3_build.

**Task F3 complete. Summary:**

**Files created (all new, nothing existing modified):**
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/docs/wire_spec.md` — the frozen layout contract, offset/size/type tables for the 8-byte header and all 8 kinds
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/wire/record.h`, `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/wire/record.cpp` — namespace jnx codec: UpdateRecord/OrderRecord/TickRecord/HelloRecord/SyncEndRecord, encode_*/decode_* with strict magic/version/kind/body_len validation and static reason strings, `encode_control` for the body-less kinds (B/G/R), and `RecordFramer` (byte-at-a-time-safe; invalid header = permanently corrupt stream with `corrupt_reason()`, per the no-resync design)
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/test/test_record.cpp` — 17 minitest tests
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/tools/gen_record_vectors.cpp` + `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/test/vectors/records.bin` (1435 bytes, 10 records: H, G, R, B, K, O, 3×U, E)
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/jnxweb/__init__.py`, `/workspace/jnx-feed/jnxweb/records.py` — 3.6-safe decoder, `decode_header`/`decode_record`/`decode_stream`, field names mirror the C++ structs exactly
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/tests/unit/test_records_py.py` — 11 pytest cases, every field of every record in records.bin hardcoded, plus a test that regex-extracts the frozen size sentence from wire_spec.md and asserts it equals `records.UPDATE_WIRE_SIZE`

**Frozen UPDATE size: 433 bytes total (8-byte header + 425-byte body).** Other sizes: ORDER 34, TICK 20, SYNC_END 34, HELLO 24, B/G/R 8. Pinned by a C++ `static_assert` that sums the per-field widths, by import-time asserts in records.py, and by the doc-quoting pytest.

**Spec decisions where the plan was loose:**
1. Header is 8 bytes: the plan's 6-byte header (magic u16, version u8, kind u8, body_len u16) plus a `reserved u16` always 0 — matches the required 8-byte header and keeps the body 64-bit-friendly.
2. Flags byte lives at the end of the static section: bit0 (LSB) = directory_seen, bit1 = order_collision_seen, bits 2–7 reserved zero.
3. T2's `last_exch_seq`/`last_update_ns` are not separate body fields — the DB takes them from the record's envelope `exch_seq`/`exch_ns` (the UPDATE is by definition the last message that touched the ticker).
4. HELLO uses one symmetric 16-byte layout for both directions (epoch u64 + last_exch_seq u64); FH sends its own last seq (0 = fresh) instead of the plan's epoch-only FH HELLO.
5. Char-tag fill: single-char fields use 0x00 for "not applicable" (`'?'` is a value, not fill); delta op `'#'` zeroes all other delta fields; `delta_order_type` uses `' '` (space) for a plain order, matching ITCH `F`'s blank convention.
6. Level slots at index >= level_count are forced to zero on the wire by the encoder regardless of struct contents (tested with poisoned buffers); decoders reject level counts > 10.
7. Stream framing is bare concatenation (header's body_len delimits, no extra length prefix); an invalid header means corrupt-and-close, no resync.

**Verification outputs (all pasted in full above):** C++ test_record 17/17 PASS under `-std=c++11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic`; gen_record_vectors wrote 1435 bytes and three runs are byte-identical (`cmp` clean); `pytest tests/unit/test_records_py.py` 11 passed; `py36check jnxweb/*` exit 0; full `python3 -m pytest -q` 214 passed.

- `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/wire/record.h`, `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/wire/record.cpp` — namespace jnx codec: UpdateRecord/OrderRecord/TickRecord/HelloRecord/SyncEndRecord, encode_*/decode_* with strict magic/version/kind/body_len validation and static reason strings, `encode_control` for the body-less kinds (B/G/R), and `RecordFramer` (byte-at-a-time-safe; invalid header = permanently corrupt stream with `corrupt_reason()`, per the no-resync design)
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/test/test_record.cpp` — 17 minitest tests
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/tools/gen_record_vectors.cpp` + `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/test/vectors/records.bin` (1435 bytes, 10 records: H, G, R, B, K, O, 3×U, E)
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/jnxweb/__init__.py`, `/workspace/jnx-feed/jnxweb/records.py` — 3.6-safe decoder, `decode_header`/`decode_record`/`decode_stream`, field names mirror the C++ structs exactly
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/tests/unit/test_records_py.py` — 11 pytest cases, every field of every record in records.bin hardcoded, plus a test that regex-extracts the frozen size sentence from wire_spec.md and asserts it equals `records.UPDATE_WIRE_SIZE`

**Frozen UPDATE size: 433 bytes total (8-byte header + 425-byte body).** Other sizes: ORDER 34, TICK 20, SYNC_END 34, HELLO 24, B/G/R 8. Pinned by a C++ `static_assert` that sums the per-field widths, by import-time asserts in records.py, and by the doc-quoting pytest.

**Spec decisions where the plan was loose:**
1. Header is 8 bytes: the plan's 6-byte header (magic u16, version u8, kind u8, body_len u16) plus a `reserved u16` always 0 — matches the required 8-byte header and keeps the body 64-bit-friendly.
2. Flags byte lives at the end of the static section: bit0 (LSB) = directory_seen, bit1 = order_collision_seen, bits 2–7 reserved zero.
3. T2's `last_exch_seq`/`last_update_ns` are not separate body fields — the DB takes them from the record's envelope `exch_seq`/`exch_ns` (the UPDATE is by definition the last message that touched the ticker).
4. HELLO uses one symmetric 16-byte layout for both directions (epoch u64 + last_exch_seq u64); FH sends its own last seq (0 = fresh) instead of the plan's epoch-only FH HELLO.
5. Char-tag fill: single-char fields use 0x00 for "not applicable" (`'?'` is a value, not fill); delta op `'#'` zeroes all other delta fields; `delta_order_type` uses `' '` (space) for a plain order, matching ITCH `F`'s blank convention.
6. Level slots at index >= level_count are forced to zero on the wire by the encoder regardless of struct contents (tested with poisoned buffers); decoders reject level counts > 10.
7. Stream framing is bare concatenation (header's body_len delimits, no extra length prefix); an invalid header means corrupt-and-close, no resync.

**Verification outputs (all pasted in full above):** C++ test_record 17/17 PASS under `-std=c++11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic`; gen_record_vectors wrote 1435 bytes and three runs are byte-identical (`cmp` clean); `pytest tests/unit/test_records_py.py` 11 passed; `py36check jnxweb/*` exit 0; full `python3 -m pytest -q` 214 passed.

result: F3 delivered — frozen 433-byte UPDATE record wire format specced in docs/wire_spec.md with matching C++ codec (cpp/wire/), Python 3.6 decoder (jnxweb/records.py), cross-language golden vectors (records.bin), and all verification commands green (17 C++ tests, 11 new pytests, 214-test suite, py36check clean, deterministic vectors).</result>

file.content:
# cpp/Makefile — plain GNU make, no recursion, no cmake.
#
# Targets:
#   all    - build all libraries/binaries (currently just prepares build dir;
#            later phases add jnxfh/jnxdb/tools binaries as BINS grows)
#   test   - build every cpp/test/test_*.cpp into cpp/build/ and run each;
#            fails (non-zero exit) if any test binary fails or any build fails
#   clean  - remove cpp/build/
#
# Adding a later main binary (e.g. jnxfh) should just mean adding its .cpp
# sources to a SRCS_jnxfh list and a rule/target following the pattern used
# for tools below — the test/build machinery here does not need to change.

CXX ?= g++
CXXFLAGS = -std=c++11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic -O2 -g -pthread
LDFLAGS = -pthread

ROOT := $(abspath $(dir $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))
BUILD := $(ROOT)/build

# Directories later phases will add sources under (kept here so `all`
# has an obvious place to grow into without restructuring the Makefile).
COMMON_DIR := $(ROOT)/common
TOOLS_DIR := $(ROOT)/tools
TEST_DIR := $(ROOT)/test

# Module ("library") sources shared by tools, main binaries and tests.
# Future phases (market/, wire/, db/, fh/) just extend MOD_DIRS; main
# binaries get their own target following the itch_replay pattern below.
MOD_DIRS := itch soup market
MOD_SRCS := $(foreach d,$(MOD_DIRS),$(wildcard $(ROOT)/$(d)/*.cpp))
MOD_OBJS := $(patsubst $(ROOT)/%.cpp,$(BUILD)/%.o,$(MOD_SRCS))

# Conservative header dependency: any header change rebuilds everything.
# Fine at this codebase size; swap for -MMD if it ever gets slow.
HDRS := $(wildcard $(ROOT)/*/*.h)

BINS := $(BUILD)/itch_replay $(BUILD)/book_dump

.PHONY: all test clean

all: $(BINS) | $(BUILD)

$(BUILD):
	mkdir -p $(BUILD)

# Pattern rule: build any object from a .cpp anywhere under cpp/, mirroring
# the relative path under build/ so future subdirs (itch/, market/, ...)
# don't collide.
$(BUILD)/%.o: $(ROOT)/%.cpp $(HDRS) | $(BUILD)
	mkdir -p $(dir $@)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -I$(ROOT) -c -o $@ $<

# --- tools --------------------------------------------------------------

$(BUILD)/itch_replay: $(BUILD)/tools/itch_replay.o $(MOD_OBJS) | $(BUILD)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -o $@ $(BUILD)/tools/itch_replay.o $(MOD_OBJS) $(LDFLAGS)

$(BUILD)/book_dump: $(BUILD)/tools/book_dump.o $(MOD_OBJS) | $(BUILD)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -o $@ $(BUILD)/tools/book_dump.o $(MOD_OBJS) $(LDFLAGS)

# --- test harness -----------------------------------------------------

# test_record.cpp belongs to the F3 wire codec task; it links against
# cpp/wire/, which is wired into this Makefile by that task (add `wire`
# to MOD_DIRS and drop this filter-out).
TEST_SRCS := $(filter-out $(TEST_DIR)/test_record.cpp,$(wildcard $(TEST_DIR)/test_*.cpp))
TEST_BINS := $(patsubst $(TEST_DIR)/%.cpp,$(BUILD)/test/%,$(TEST_SRCS))

$(BUILD)/test/%: $(TEST_DIR)/%.cpp $(MOD_OBJS) $(HDRS) | $(BUILD)
	mkdir -p $(dir $@)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -I$(ROOT) -o $@ $< $(MOD_OBJS) $(LDFLAGS)

test: $(TEST_BINS)
	@for t in $(TEST_BINS); do \
		echo "=== running $$t ==="; \
		$$t || exit 1; \
	done

clean:
	rm -rf $(BUILD)

LDFLAGS = -pthread

ROOT := $(abspath $(dir $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))
BUILD := $(ROOT)/build

MOD_DIRS := itch soup market

MOD_OBJS := $(patsubst $(ROOT)/%.cpp,$(BUILD)/%.o,$(MOD_SRCS))

HDRS := $(wildcard $(ROOT)/*/*.h)

BINS := $(BUILD)/itch_replay $(BUILD)/book_dump

.PHONY: all test clean

all: $(BINS) | $(BUILD)

$(BUILD):
	mkdir -p $(BUILD)

# Pattern rule: build any object from a .cpp anywhere under cpp/, mirroring
# the relative path under build/ so future subdirs (itch/, market/, ...)
# don't collide.
$(BUILD)/%.o: $(ROOT)/%.cpp $(HDRS) | $(BUILD)
	mkdir -p $(dir $@)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -I$(ROOT) -c -o $@ $<

	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -I$(ROOT) -o $@ $< $(MOD_OBJS) $(LDFLAGS)

test: $(TEST_BINS)
	@for t in $(TEST_BINS); do \
		echo "=== running $$t ==="; \
		$$t || exit 1; \
	done

	rm -rf $(BUILD)

# cpp/Makefile — plain GNU make, no recursion, no cmake.
#
# Targets:
#   all    - build all libraries/binaries (currently just prepares build dir;
#            later phases add jnxfh/jnxdb/tools binaries as BINS grows)
#   test   - build every cpp/test/test_*.cpp into cpp/build/ and run each;
#            fails (non-zero exit) if any test binary fails or any build fails
#   clean  - remove cpp/build/
#
# Adding a later main binary (e.g. jnxfh) should just mean adding its .cpp
# sources to a SRCS_jnxfh list and a rule/target following the pattern used
# for tools below — the test/build machinery here does not need to change.

CXX ?= g++
CXXFLAGS = -std=c++11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic -O2 -g -pthread
LDFLAGS = -pthread

ROOT := $(abspath $(dir $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))
BUILD := $(ROOT)/build

# Directories later phases will add sources under (kept here so `all`
# has an obvious place to grow into without restructuring the Makefile).
COMMON_DIR := $(ROOT)/common
TOOLS_DIR := $(ROOT)/tools
TEST_DIR := $(ROOT)/test

# Module ("library") sources shared by tools, main binaries and tests.
# Future phases (market/, wire/, db/, fh/) just extend MOD_DIRS; main
# binaries get their own target following the itch_replay pattern below.
MOD_DIRS := itch soup market wire
MOD_SRCS := $(foreach d,$(MOD_DIRS),$(wildcard $(ROOT)/$(d)/*.cpp))
MOD_OBJS := $(patsubst $(ROOT)/%.cpp,$(BUILD)/%.o,$(MOD_SRCS))

# Conservative header dependency: any header change rebuilds everything.
# Fine at this codebase size; swap for -MMD if it ever gets slow.
HDRS := $(wildcard $(ROOT)/*/*.h)

BINS := $(BUILD)/itch_replay $(BUILD)/book_dump $(BUILD)/gen_record_vectors

.PHONY: all test clean

all: $(BINS) | $(BUILD)

$(BUILD):
	mkdir -p $(BUILD)

# Pattern rule: build any object from a .cpp anywhere under cpp/, mirroring
# the relative path under build/ so future subdirs (itch/, market/, ...)
# don't collide.
$(BUILD)/%.o: $(ROOT)/%.cpp $(HDRS) | $(BUILD)
	mkdir -p $(dir $@)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -I$(ROOT) -c -o $@ $<

# --- tools --------------------------------------------------------------

$(BUILD)/itch_replay: $(BUILD)/tools/itch_replay.o $(MOD_OBJS) | $(BUILD)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -o $@ $(BUILD)/tools/itch_replay.o $(MOD_OBJS) $(LDFLAGS)

$(BUILD)/book_dump: $(BUILD)/tools/book_dump.o $(MOD_OBJS) | $(BUILD)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -o $@ $(BUILD)/tools/book_dump.o $(MOD_OBJS) $(LDFLAGS)

# --- test harness -----------------------------------------------------

# test_record.cpp belongs to the F3 wire codec task; it links against
# cpp/wire/, which is wired into this Makefile by that task (add `wire`
# to MOD_DIRS and drop this filter-out).
TEST_SRCS := $(filter-out $(TEST_DIR)/test_record.cpp,$(wildcard $(TEST_DIR)/test_*.cpp))
TEST_BINS := $(patsubst $(TEST_DIR)/%.cpp,$(BUILD)/test/%,$(TEST_SRCS))

$(BUILD)/test/%: $(TEST_DIR)/%.cpp $(MOD_OBJS) $(HDRS) | $(BUILD)
	mkdir -p $(dir $@)
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -I$(ROOT) -o $@ $< $(MOD_OBJS) $(LDFLAGS)

test: $(TEST_BINS)
	@for t in $(TEST_BINS); do \
		echo "=== running $$t ==="; \
		$$t || exit 1; \
	done

clean:
	rm -rf $(BUILD)

content.text:
**F2 and F3 are both complete, verified, and committed.**

**F2 — C++ market core (`5676c49`).** The critical parity gate passed: `diff -r` between the Python-prototype state dump and the C++ `book_dump` output is **empty on the full 222k-message golden sample** (I re-verified this myself from a clean rebuild, not just trusting the agent). Full-sample state: 144 books, 50,736 live orders, 67,902 executions, zero orphans/collisions; the C++ replay of the whole day takes ~0.34 s vs the prototype's ~0.6 min-range pace — the performance headroom we wanted from the port. The agent found a few places where the plan text and the prototype disagreed and correctly let the prototype win, notably: books are keyed by orderbook_id alone (group is refdata-sourced), price levels track qty only (order_count is derived from the order store), and an order-number collision *replaces* the stale order rather than just warning. I'll fold those clarifications into the plan text before F4 picks up the schema.

**F3 — frozen wire format (`53ebcdb`).** `docs/wire_spec.md` is now the contract: **UPDATE record frozen at 433 bytes** (8-byte header + 425-byte body), with ORDER/TICK/HELLO/SYNC records for the recovery protocol. It's pinned three ways — a C++ `static_assert`, import-time asserts in the Python decoder, and a pytest that literally regex-extracts the size from the spec document and compares. Cross-language proof: `records.bin` is generated by the C++ encoder and every field of every record is asserted from the Python 3.6-safe decoder (`jnxweb/records.py`, py36check-clean). One sensible deviation: HELLO is symmetric both directions (epoch + last_seq), which simplifies the F4/F5 handshake.

Combined state: 43 C++ tests and 214 Python tests green, whole build still clean under `-std=c++11 -Werror -pedantic`. One session-limit interruption hit both agents mid-task; they resumed from their transcripts without losing work.

Next up per the plan: **F4 (`jnxdb` process)** — it only depends on F3, so it can start immediately; **F5 (`jnxfh`)** depends on F4's ingest protocol, so I'd run F4 first, then F5, reusing the same two agents. Ready to dispatch F4 on your go.

input.old_string: Primary key everywhere: **`ticker`** = 4-char SICC orderbook id (e.g. `8306`). Japannext runs one book per (ticker, group) where group ∈ {`DAY `, `NGHT`, `DAYX`, `DAYU`}; a single feed/session carries one group in practice, but the schema keys on **(ticker, group)** with ticker as the primary lookup — a plain ticker query returns all groups (usually one).
input.new_string:
Primary key everywhere: **`ticker`** = 4-char SICC orderbook id (e.g. `8306`). Japannext runs one book per (ticker, group) where group ∈ {`DAY `, `NGHT`, `DAYX`, `DAYU`}; a single feed/session carries one group in practice, but the schema keys on **(ticker, group)** with ticker as the primary lookup — a plain ticker query returns all groups (usually one).

**F2 outcome notes (prototype semantics won, schema reads accordingly):** the C++ market core (like the prototype) keys books/tape by **ticker alone** — the `group` column is sourced from refdata and may be blank for auto-created books; per-level `order_count` is **derived from the live-order store**, not tracked in levels; an order-number collision **replaces** the stale order (and increments the collision counter). The DB tables below simply mirror what UPDATE records carry — these notes matter to anyone reasoning about semantics, not to the DB implementation.

input.summary: Assign F4: jnxdb in-memory database process
input.message:
Next task: F4 — the jnxdb in-memory database process. You built the F3 wire codec, so you know the record format; jnxdb consumes it. Rules: work in /workspace/jnx-feed, NO git commands. No other agent is running now: you OWN cpp/Makefile again and may use `make -C cpp` freely (your F3 module is already wired in: MOD_DIRS = itch soup market wire; gen_record_vectors is in BINS).

FIRST read:
- /workspace/jnx-feed/JNX_PLAN2.md — Phase F4 (all subsections), §1 (architecture + restart matrix — jnxdb's role), §2 (the 5 tables incl. the new "F2 outcome notes"), §3 (protocol kinds and semantics).
- docs/wire_spec.md — your own frozen spec; jnxdb must follow it exactly.
- cpp/wire/record.h (your codec — reuse, do not duplicate), cpp/common/{log.h,cfg.h,time.h}.

Deliverables:
1. cpp/db/tables.h/.cpp — the 5 tables + meta + tick_tables over std::map/unordered_map. apply_update(const UpdateRecord&): upsert static/state/book_agg/trades sections wholesale keyed by (ticker,group); mutate orders table from the delta section (op A insert incl. order_type; E decrement qty_remaining, erase at 0; D erase; U erase orig_order_number + insert order_number inheriting nothing — the record's delta carries all needed fields; '#' = no order mutation); append tape ring (last 50) when trigger=='E'; update meta.last_exch_seq. Dup guard: same epoch AND exch_seq <= meta.last_exch_seq -> drop, count, WARN (rate-limited: first + every 1000th). apply_order/apply_tick for sync/recovery rows. reset(). dump_state(std::function<void(const unsigned char*, size_t)> sink): SYNC_BEGIN, all TICK rows, all ORDER rows, one UPDATE per (ticker,group) with trigger '#' and delta op '#', SYNC_END with meta — deterministic order (sorted).
2. cpp/db/ingest.h/.cpp — UDS listener (cfg key sock=, default /tmp/jnx-db.sock for dev; unlink stale path on startup and on clean shutdown). ONE FH connection at a time; a new accept kicks the old with a WARN. RecordFramer per connection. Protocol: HELLO in -> reply HELLO(epoch, last_exch_seq) (0,0 when empty); GET_STATE -> dump_state to the same socket; RESET -> tables.reset(); SYNC_BEGIN...SYNC_END bracket (on END: adopt meta; if the connection drops between BEGIN and END, discard: reset() + WARN — partial sync must never survive; live UPDATEs arriving OUTSIDE a sync bracket are normal flow, never trigger the wipe rule); UPDATE/ORDER/TICK -> apply. Corrupt frame (framer corrupt_reason) -> log ERROR, close that connection, keep tables.
3. cpp/db/query.h/.cpp — TCP listener 127.0.0.1, cfg query_port= default 26401, SO_REUSEADDR. Line-based text protocol, response terminated by a lone "." line: PING->PONG; GET <ticker> (all tables merged, key=value lines, one block per group if several); BOOK <ticker> (levels best-first, aligned columns, prices rendered /10 with 1 decimal); ORDERS <ticker> (live orders sorted by order_number); TRADES <ticker> (summary + tape newest-first); TABLE static|state|trades (CSV with header, sorted); STATS (session, epoch, last_exch_seq, updates_applied, dups_dropped, orders_live, books, ticks, sync counters); unknown ticker -> "ERR unknown"; unknown cmd -> "ERR badcmd". Per-connection write buffering drained on POLLOUT — a slow query client must never stall ingest.
4. cpp/db/jnxdb_main.cpp -> BINS jnxdb: SINGLE-THREADED poll() loop over {uds listener, uds conn, tcp listener, tcp conns}; cfg file + --key=value overrides (jnx::Cfg); SIGINT/SIGTERM -> clean shutdown (close, unlink sock, INFO log); 5s periodic INFO stats line.
5. tools/dbquery.py — Python 3.6-safe (passes tools/py36check.py), argparse: --port (default 26401) plus command words joined into one line; prints response body without the trailing "."; exit 1 on ERR.
6. Extend jnxweb/records.py with 3.6-safe ENCODE functions for all record kinds (mirror your decode; assert encode(decode(x))==x against records.bin in the existing pytest file or a new one) — needed by the python test feeder and later phases.
7. cpp/test/test_tables.cpp — apply semantics per delta op, dup rejection (same epoch old seq dropped; new epoch accepted), tape ring cap, dump_state->fresh Tables re-apply->identical (compare via a canonical serialize or field walk), reset.
8. tests/integration/test_jnxdb.py — pytest (python3.14 ok, but keep it 3.6-clean anyway): starts cpp/build/jnxdb with a temp sock/port, connects a raw UDS socket as fake FH: HELLO handshake asserted; RESET+SYNC dump (a few TICK/ORDER/UPDATE '#' rows)+SYNC_END; live UPDATEs incl. an out-of-seq dup (assert dropped via STATS); GET/BOOK/ORDERS/TRADES/STATS/TABLE responses asserted; partial-sync-then-disconnect wipes; corrupt frame closes ingest but query still works; kill and SIGTERM paths. Use jnxweb.records encoders. Make sockets/ports collision-safe (tmpdir + ephemeral port: pass query_port=0? simpler: pick a random high port and retry on bind failure — implement retry in the test, not the server).

VERIFICATION (run all, paste tails):
  make -C cpp clean && make -C cpp test && make -C cpp all
  python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_jnxdb.py -q
  python3 tools/py36check.py tools/dbquery.py jnxweb/records.py
  python3 -m pytest -q          # whole suite green
  Manual smoke (paste): start jnxdb, dbquery PING / STATS, ctrl via kill -TERM, confirm clean shutdown log + sock unlinked.

HARD RULES: C++11 only, -Werror -pedantic clean, zero external deps, single-threaded jnxdb (no std::thread in the server), no git. Final message: files created/changed, test tails, STATS output sample, any spec ambiguities you resolved.

1. cpp/db/tables.h/.cpp — the 5 tables + meta + tick_tables over std::map/unordered_map. apply_update(const UpdateRecord&): upsert static/state/book_agg/trades sections wholesale keyed by (ticker,group); mutate orders table from the delta section (op A insert incl. order_type; E decrement qty_remaining, erase at 0; D erase; U erase orig_order_number + insert order_number inheriting nothing — the record's delta carries all needed fields; '#' = no order mutation); append tape ring (last 50) when trigger=='E'; update meta.last_exch_seq. Dup guard: same epoch AND exch_seq <= meta.last_exch_seq -> drop, count, WARN (rate-limited: first + every 1000th). apply_order/apply_tick for sync/recovery rows. reset(). dump_state(std::function<void(const unsigned char*, size_t)> sink): SYNC_BEGIN, all TICK rows, all ORDER rows, one UPDATE per (ticker,group) with trigger '#' and delta op '#', SYNC_END with meta — deterministic order (sorted).
2. cpp/db/ingest.h/.cpp — UDS listener (cfg key sock=, default /tmp/jnx-db.sock for dev; unlink stale path on startup and on clean shutdown). ONE FH connection at a time; a new accept kicks the old with a WARN. RecordFramer per connection. Protocol: HELLO in -> reply HELLO(epoch, last_exch_seq) (0,0 when empty); GET_STATE -> dump_state to the same socket; RESET -> tables.reset(); SYNC_BEGIN...SYNC_END bracket (on END: adopt meta; if the connection drops between BEGIN and END, discard: reset() + WARN — partial sync must never survive; live UPDATEs arriving OUTSIDE a sync bracket are normal flow, never trigger the wipe rule); UPDATE/ORDER/TICK -> apply. Corrupt frame (framer corrupt_reason) -> log ERROR, close that connection, keep tables.
3. cpp/db/query.h/.cpp — TCP listener 127.0.0.1, cfg query_port= default 26401, SO_REUSEADDR. Line-based text protocol, response terminated by a lone "." line: PING->PONG; GET <ticker> (all tables merged, key=value lines, one block per group if several); BOOK <ticker> (levels best-first, aligned columns, prices rendered /10 with 1 decimal); ORDERS <ticker> (live orders sorted by order_number); TRADES <ticker> (summary + tape newest-first); TABLE static|state|trades (CSV with header, sorted); STATS (session, epoch, last_exch_seq, updates_applied, dups_dropped, orders_live, books, ticks, sync counters); unknown ticker -> "ERR unknown"; unknown cmd -> "ERR badcmd". Per-connection write buffering drained on POLLOUT — a slow query client must never stall ingest.
4. cpp/db/jnxdb_main.cpp -> BINS jnxdb: SINGLE-THREADED poll() loop over {uds listener, uds conn, tcp listener, tcp conns}; cfg file + --key=value overrides (jnx::Cfg); SIGINT/SIGTERM -> clean shutdown (close, unlink sock, INFO log); 5s periodic INFO stats line.
5. tools/dbquery.py — Python 3.6-safe (passes tools/py36check.py), argparse: --port (default 26401) plus command words joined into one line; prints response body without the trailing "."; exit 1 on ERR.
6. Extend jnxweb/records.py with 3.6-safe ENCODE functions for all record kinds (mirror your decode; assert encode(decode(x))==x against records.bin in the existing pytest file or a new one) — needed by the python test feeder and later phases.
7. cpp/test/test_tables.cpp — apply semantics per delta op, dup rejection (same epoch old seq dropped; new epoch accepted), tape ring cap, dump_state->fresh Tables re-apply->identical (compare via a canonical serialize or field walk), reset.
8. tests/integration/test_jnxdb.py — pytest (python3.14 ok, but keep it 3.6-clean anyway): starts cpp/build/jnxdb with a temp sock/port, connects a raw UDS socket as fake FH: HELLO handshake asserted; RESET+SYNC dump (a few TICK/ORDER/UPDATE '#' rows)+SYNC_END; live UPDATEs incl. an out-of-seq dup (assert dropped via STATS); GET/BOOK/ORDERS/TRADES/STATS/TABLE responses asserted; partial-sync-then-disconnect wipes; corrupt frame closes ingest but query still works; kill and SIGTERM paths. Use jnxweb.records encoders. Make sockets/ports collision-safe (tmpdir + ephemeral port: pass query_port=0? simpler: pick a random high port and retry on bind failure — implement retry in the test, not the server).

VERIFICATION (run all, paste tails):
  make -C cpp clean && make -C cpp test && make -C cpp all
  python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_jnxdb.py -q
  python3 tools/py36check.py tools/dbquery.py jnxweb/records.py
  python3 -m pytest -q          # whole suite green
  Manual smoke (paste): start jnxdb, dbquery PING / STATS, ctrl via kill -TERM, confirm clean shutdown log + sock unlinked.

HARD RULES: C++11 only, -Werror -pedantic clean, zero external deps, single-threaded jnxdb (no std::thread in the server), no git. Final message: files created/changed, test tails, STATS output sample, any spec ambiguities you resolved.

**Spec ambiguities I resolved:**
1. Dup guard requires `updates_applied > 0` — a fresh empty DB (epoch 0, seq 0) never drops the first record even if the FH is also at epoch 0.
2. `reset()` zeroes meta and counters but preserves `syncs_completed`/`syncs_discarded` so a partial-sync wipe remains observable in STATS afterwards.
3. Delta `E` with unknown order number is silently ignored (no counter) — the FH is authoritative for T3; the DB just mirrors.
4. Delta `E` also refreshes the order's price from `delta_price` (harmless; the delta carries the post-op state per my F3 spec).
5. Sync-mode UPDATEs (`in_sync=true`) bypass the dup guard AND meta adoption — meta comes only from SYNC_END; this is what makes multiple dump rows with equal exch_seq applicable.
6. `ORDERS <ticker>` reports "unknown" only if the ticker appears in no book row and has no orders; a known ticker with zero live orders returns just the header.
7. Corrupt stream mid-sync counts as a disconnect inside the bracket → same partial-sync wipe rule applies.
8. GET_STATE while mid-bracket is not blocked (single-threaded loop makes it atomic anyway); nested SYNC_BEGIN logs a WARN and stays in-bracket.

content.text:
**F4 is done, verified, and committed (`ac952db`).** The `jnxdb` process is real and running:

- **Single-threaded by design** — one `poll()` loop over the UDS ingest and the TCP query listener, so every record applies atomically with zero locking.
- **Full protocol implemented**: HELLO handshake (epoch + last seq), GET_STATE recovery dump, RESET, and the SYNC bracket with the safety rule that a connection dropping *mid-sync* wipes the partial state (verified by test), while a drop during live flow keeps everything.
- **Query interface** works end-to-end: `PING/GET/BOOK/ORDERS/TRADES/TABLE/STATS` over TCP with `tools/dbquery.py` as the probe; slow query clients are buffered (4 MB cap) and can't stall ingest.
- My independent verification: 59 C++ tests + 232 Python tests green from a clean rebuild, the 12 jnxdb integration tests pass in ~1 s, and a manual smoke — start, PING→PONG, STATS, SIGTERM → exit 0 with a clean-shutdown log and the socket unlinked.

The agent resolved a handful of spec gaps sensibly and documented them (e.g. the dup-guard never fires on a fresh empty DB; sync-mode rows bypass the dup guard and meta comes only from SYNC_END — which is exactly what makes recovery dumps replayable; a corrupt stream mid-sync counts as a mid-sync disconnect and wipes). Nothing there conflicts with the plan.

Next is **F5 — the `jnxfh` feed handler itself**: Soup client with reconnect-resume, GLIMPSE bootstrap, the market core driving one UPDATE per exchange message into jnxdb + multicast, tested live against the Python simulator with the exact golden numbers (216,346 published updates, gap-free). It's the biggest task so far; I'd give it to Agent A (it wrote the codecs and market core). Say go and I'll dispatch.

1. /workspace/jnx-feed/JNX_PLAN2.md — Phase F5 (all subsections, esp. boundary conditions + pitfalls), §1 (architecture AND the restart-matrix table — you implement rows 1–4), §3 (protocol kinds).
2. docs/wire_spec.md — the record layouts you must emit.
3. Prototype references (semantics to mirror): jnxfeed/soup/session.py (Soup client state machine), jnxfeed/glimpse.py, jnxfeed/handler.py (bootstrap orchestration), jnxfeed/net/reactor.py.
4. The simulator you will test against: jnxfeed/sim/exchange.py + jnxfeed/__main__.py / jnxfeed/cli — find the exact CLI to serve a .itch fixture as Soup ITCH + GLIMPSE servers (it exists from the prototype; do not modify it — if the CLI lacks an option you need, say so in your final message rather than hacking it).

Deliverables:
1. cpp/fh/reactor.h/.cpp — minimal poll() loop + monotonic timers (mirror prototype reactor semantics; ~150 LoC).
2. cpp/fh/soupclient.h/.cpp — sans-I/O Soup client session state machine (login with username/password/session/requested seq; self-counted seq from Login-Accepted value; client heartbeat after 1 s idle; server dead after 15 s silence) + TCP glue with reconnect (backoff 1 s doubling capped 10 s) re-logging-in with SAME session at next expected seq. Handle: Login Rejected codes A/S -> log ERROR + terminate cleanly (exit nonzero, no retry storm); Z end-of-session -> clean stop. Unit-test the state machine against scripted byte sequences using your SoupFramer (no sockets in unit tests).
3. cpp/fh/glimpse.h/.cpp — GLIMPSE bootstrap: Soup session (blank requested session, seq 1) applying snapshot ITCH messages to a Market until G; returns next live seq from G.
4. cpp/fh/publish.h/.cpp — (a) the record builder deferred from F3: make_update(const Market&, const ApplyResult&, envelope fields) -> UpdateRecord assembling full ticker state: static+state from refdata, top-10 levels + totals from the book (order_count derived from the order store, same as book_dump does), trade summary from tape, delta from ApplyResult; (b) DB link: UDS connect + HELLO handshake, blocking framed writes with SO_SNDTIMEO=5s; on connect/write failure -> background reconnect loop (1 s) while live flow continues (multicast keeps going, db_connected=false gauge); on reconnect: HELLO, and if DB is empty or epoch mismatches ours -> RESET + full SYNC dump built from our own Market (SYNC_BEGIN, all TICK rows, all ORDER rows, one UPDATE per book trigger '#' delta '#', SYNC_END) then resume live; (c) multicast sender: one sendto per UPDATE, cfg mcast group/port/ttl/interface, IP_MULTICAST_LOOP=1.
5. cpp/fh/recover.h/.cpp — startup decision tree (plan §1 restart matrix): connect DB, HELLO; if DB has state -> GET_STATE, rebuild Market from the dump (TICK->tick tables, ORDER rows->order store+levels, UPDATE '#' rows->refdata/state/trade summary restore), adopt session+epoch, Soup login at last_seq+1 with that session; else per --bootstrap=glimpse|replay: glimpse -> snapshot into fresh Market, RESET+SYNC to DB, ITCH login at seq N; replay -> fresh Market, RESET (+empty SYNC) to DB, ITCH login seq 1. You will need a Market restore path — add minimal injection methods to cpp/market (clearly named, e.g. restore_order/restore_book_row) rather than bit-twiddling from outside; keep them out of the live apply path.
6. cpp/fh/jnxfh_main.cpp -> BINS jnxfh: cfg + --key=value (itch_host/itch_port/glimpse_host/glimpse_port/user/pass/session/requested db_sock/mcast_group/mcast_port/mcast_ttl/mcast_if/bootstrap/require_db); live loop: soup packet -> itch decode -> market.apply -> if publishable: make_update -> DB write + mcast send; epoch = now_ns() at cold bootstrap, ADOPTED from DB on recovery; pub_seq starts 1, +1 per published UPDATE. PUBLISH RULE (pinned): every applied exchange message publishes exactly one UPDATE EXCEPT T (timestamp) messages, which publish nothing — on the golden sample: 222189 applied, 216346 published. 5 s INFO stats line (msgs/s, exch seq, pub_seq, books, orders, db_connected). SIGINT/SIGTERM clean shutdown.
7. tools/mcast_spy.py — Python 3.6-safe (py36check clean), joins the group (--group/--port/--iface), decodes via jnxweb.records, default prints one line per UPDATE (pub_seq, trigger, ticker, best bid/ask, last trade); --stats mode: totals + pub_seq gap count; --until-idle N (default 3 s): exit after N seconds without a datagram, printing the stats summary.
8. tools/compare_db_dump.py — python3 dev tool: pulls jnxdb state via the query port (TABLE/ORDERS/etc through tools/dbquery.py logic or raw socket) and compares against a book_dump canonical dump directory over their overlapping content: live orders (full), per-book top-10 levels + totals, trade summaries (cum_qty/cum_turnover/trade_count/last_*), static/state fields. Prints per-section OK/MISMATCH with first diffs; exit 0 only if all match.
9. cpp/test/test_soupclient.cpp (+ any other unit tests) and tests/integration/test_jnxfh.py — pytest: starts the Python simulator on the HEAD fixture (tests/fixtures/sample_udp_head.itch) + jnxdb + jnxfh (bootstrap=replay), waits for completion (Z / idle), asserts: dbquery STATS last_exch_seq and updates_applied match expectation; mcast_spy stats gap-free; compare_db_dump.py clean vs cpp/build/book_dump of the same fixture; plus a GLIMPSE-bootstrap variant if the simulator supports serving GLIMPSE for the fixture (check its CLI; if not feasible, note it — F6 will cover it). Use collision-safe ports/socket paths.

VERIFICATION (run all, paste outputs):
  make -C cpp clean && make -C cpp test && make -C cpp all
  python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_jnxfh.py -q
  python3 tools/py36check.py tools/mcast_spy.py
  python3 -m pytest -q            # whole suite still green
  FULL golden run (paste the numbers):
    cpp/build/jnxdb --sock=/tmp/f5.sock --query_port=27501 &
    python3 -m jnxfeed <sim args for tests/fixtures/sample_udp.itch, ports of your choosing> &
    cpp/build/jnxfh --itch_host=127.0.0.1 ... --db_sock=/tmp/f5.sock --bootstrap=replay --mcast_group=239.192.1.1 --mcast_port=26400 --mcast_if=127.0.0.1 &
    python3 tools/mcast_spy.py --stats --until-idle 3        # MUST: updates=216346, gaps=0
    python3 tools/dbquery.py --port 27501 STATS              # MUST: last_exch_seq=234751, updates_applied=216346, dups_dropped=0
    cpp/build/book_dump tests/fixtures/sample_udp.itch /tmp/f5_ref && python3 tools/compare_db_dump.py --port 27501 /tmp/f5_ref   # all sections OK
  Then clean up all processes.

HARD RULES: C++11 only, -Werror -pedantic clean, zero external deps, no git. jnxfh single-threaded (reactor + timers; the DB reconnect "loop" is a reactor timer, not a thread). DB write ordering: DB write BEFORE mcast send (DB is authoritative). Never publish during GLIMPSE application or recovery rebuild — those rows reach the DB as the SYNC dump; live publishing (and pub_seq) starts after. Final message: files created/changed, unit+integration tails, the full-golden-run numbers, simulator CLI you used, any deviations/limitations.

jnxfh:              exit 0, published=216346, decode_errors=0, db_write_fails=0; full day in ~2.5 s
```
All processes cleaned up.

**Deviations / limitations:**
1. **last_exch_seq is 222189, not 234751.** The simulator numbers sequences 1..N over the fixture (`messages[seq-1]` in jnxfeed/sim/exchange.py) with no way to set a base offset (the sample's original stream started at 12562). Per instructions I did not modify the sim; the published-update count (216346 = 222189 − 5843 T) matches the plan exactly.
2. mcast_spy's `--stats` mode uses a header+envelope-only fast parse and a large SO_RCVBUF — full Python decode cannot keep pace with a max-speed replay (the first run showed 683 receiver-side drops; now 0). Non-stats (per-line) mode still fully decodes.
3. Ticker-less triggers (system-wide/group-only `S`, `L`) publish UPDATEs with an empty ticker as designed ("one UPDATE per message"); jnxdb holds them as (""|group) pseudo-rows, which compare_db_dump skips deliberately.
4. GLIMPSE integration variant asserts orders+books sections + last_exch_seq; trade history legitimately differs after snapshot bootstrap (GLIMPSE carries open orders, not past trades) — full trade parity across a mid-session GLIMPSE join is F6's scenario (d).
5. On DB reconnect the FH always does RESET + full SYNC when the DB's (epoch, last_seq) doesn't exactly match its own — a superset of the plan's "empty/epoch-mismatched" rule, safe and idempotent.

result: F5 delivered — cpp/build/jnxfh (reactor, soup client with resume, GLIMPSE bootstrap, DB recovery, one UPDATE per non-T message, DB-then-mcast publish) plus mcast_spy/compare_db_dump tools and tests; full golden run gives updates=216346 gaps=0, DB last_exch_seq=222189 updates_applied=216346 dups=0, compare all sections OK, 65 C++ + 234 Python tests green (note: last_exch_seq is 222189 not 234751 because the simulator numbers fixtures from seq 1 with no offset option).</result>

content.text:
**F5 is done, independently verified, and committed (`f09f56b`).** The three-process pipeline now runs end-to-end: I reran the full golden day myself — simulator → `jnxfh` → `jnxdb` + multicast — and got exactly the expected numbers:

- **Multicast:** 216,346 updates (222,189 messages minus the 5,843 `T` timestamps), pub_seq contiguous 1→216346, **zero gaps**, single epoch.
- **Database:** `last_exch_seq=222189`, `updates_applied=216346`, zero dup drops, 50,736 live orders, 156 books; `compare_db_dump.py` reports **all five sections OK** against the `book_dump` reference.
- **F2 parity re-verified**: F5 had to extend the market core (per-level order counts, O(1) side totals, restore-injection methods for recovery), so I reran the full Python-vs-C++ dump diff — still empty. 65 C++ + 234 Python tests green. The C++ FH chews through the whole day in ~2.5 s.

Notable findings from the agent, all reasonable:
- `last_exch_seq` is 222189 rather than the pcap's original 234751 because the prototype simulator renumbers fixture messages from seq 1 (no offset option). The plan's verification text assumed original numbering — the count-based assertions are the meaningful ones and they match exactly.
- The DB-reconnect rule was made stricter than planned: any (epoch, seq) mismatch triggers RESET + full re-sync, which is a safe superset.
- GLIMPSE-bootstrap runs can't match *trade history* (snapshots carry open orders, not past trades) — expected, and it's precisely F6's scenario (d) invariant to pin down.

What remains per plan: **F6** (kill/restart recovery proof — FH restart from DB, DB restart re-sync, exchange drop resume, GLIMPSE cold start, dual kill), then F7 (the Python web GUI) and F8 (hardening/bench/ops). Ready to dispatch F6 on your go — Agent A has all the context for it.

input.description: F6 restart/recovery end-to-end tests
input.prompt:
You are implementing task F6 — restart & recovery end-to-end proof — for a market-data feed-handler project in the git repo /workspace/jnx-feed. Do NOT run any git commands (the orchestrator commits). All prior phases (F0–F5) are committed and verified working.

CONTEXT (system already built and green):
- `cpp/build/jnxdb` — single-threaded in-memory DB: UDS ingest (records per docs/wire_spec.md; HELLO/GET_STATE/RESET/SYNC protocol, partial-sync wipe on mid-bracket disconnect), TCP query port (line protocol: PING/GET/BOOK/ORDERS/TRADES/TABLE/STATS, "." terminator). Flags: --sock= --query_port=.
- `cpp/build/jnxfh` — feed handler: SoupBinTCP client with reconnect-resume, GLIMPSE bootstrap, startup decision tree (recover-from-DB via GET_STATE and resume at last_seq+1 / --bootstrap=glimpse / --bootstrap=replay), publishes one UPDATE per non-T exchange message: DB write first, then multicast. Flags: --itch_host --itch_port --glimpse_host --glimpse_port --user --pass --db_sock --bootstrap --mcast_group --mcast_port --mcast_if. Exit 0 on session end (Z), exit 5 on login reject. On DB connection loss it keeps running/multicasting and resyncs (RESET+full SYNC) when the DB returns.
- Python prototype simulator (proven): `python3 -m jnxfeed.sim --itch-file <fixture> --itch-port N --glimpse-port M` (defaults user TEST / pass SECRET / session SIM0000001; check `python3 -m jnxfeed.sim --help` and jnxfeed/sim/exchange.py for pacing/disconnect options — READ ITS CLI before designing; it supports requested-seq logins and GLIMPSE snapshots at a cut point; it may support scripted disconnects from the prototype plan T6.1 — verify what exists. Do NOT modify the simulator or any jnxfeed/ prototype code).
- Tools: tools/dbquery.py (--port, command), tools/mcast_spy.py (--group --port --iface --stats --until-idle N), tools/compare_db_dump.py (--port <query_port> <book_dump_dir>), cpp/build/book_dump <fixture> <outdir> (canonical reference dumps).
- Fixtures: tests/fixtures/sample_udp_head.itch (2000 msgs, fast) and tests/fixtures/sample_udp.itch (222189 msgs). Note: the sim renumbers sequences from 1.
- Dev env: python3 = 3.14, gcc 15, no docker. C++ rules if you touch any C++: C++11 only, -Werror -pedantic clean (make -C cpp test must stay green). Python style rules for anything under jnxweb/ or tools/ that targets production: must pass tools/py36check.py; dev-only test tooling may be modern but keep it simple.

FIRST read:
1. /workspace/jnx-feed/JNX_PLAN2.md — Phase F6 (objective, steps, boundary conditions, verification, pitfalls) and §1's restart-matrix table (the invariants you must prove).
2. docs/wire_spec.md (record kinds), and skim tests/integration/test_jnxfh.py (existing orchestration patterns to reuse/extract).
3. The simulator CLI/source as noted above.

DELIVERABLES:
1. tools/run_e2e.py — process orchestrator (dev tool, python3): starts sim + jnxdb + jnxfh with collision-safe ports/socket paths (derive from PID or random with bind-retry), supports scenario actions triggered on OBSERVED PROGRESS (poll dbquery STATS for updates_applied / last_exch_seq thresholds — never bare sleeps): kill_fh@pct, kill_db@pct (SIGKILL), drop_exchange@pct (if the sim supports scripted disconnect use it; otherwise implement a TCP proxy in run_e2e.py that sits between jnxfh and the sim and closes the connection at the trigger — a ~60-line single-connection forwarder is fine and also future-proof), restart after a configurable delay, wait for session end, then collect: final DB dumps (TABLE static/state/trades + ORDERS + STATS via the query port), mcast spy stats, all process logs into a results dir. --scenario <name> --fixture <path> [--paced] CLI; prints PASS/FAIL and paths. Use the sim's pacing option if available so kills land mid-stream deterministically; at max speed the head fixture completes too fast.
2. tests/integration/test_recovery.py — pytest, one test per scenario, comparing FINAL DB state (canonical text pulled via the query port, normalized) against the uninterrupted-baseline run of the same fixture:
   a. FH SIGKILLed at ~40% progress, restarted: log line proves resume via DB recovery at last_seq+1 (assert on jnxfh log content), dups_dropped==0, final state identical to baseline.
   b. DB SIGKILLed at ~40%, restarted 2 s later: FH keeps multicasting during the outage (mcast updates keep counting), resync happens (jnxdb STATS syncs_completed >= 1), final state identical.
   c. Exchange connection dropped at ~40% (sim scripted-disconnect or the proxy): Soup resume, no duplicate publishes (pub_seq contiguous in spy stats), final state identical.
   d. Cold start with GLIMPSE bootstrap from a mid-fixture cut point (use the sim's GLIMPSE cut support): final orders/books/static/state identical to baseline; trades EXPECTEDLY differ (snapshot has no trade history) — assert orders+books+static identical AND document/assert the exact expected trades difference (cum stats only reflect post-snapshot trades).
   e. FH AND DB both SIGKILLed at ~40%, both restarted (DB first): FH finds empty DB, bootstraps per its --bootstrap mode against the still-running sim (pick the mode that can actually reproduce baseline state on this sim — if the sim's requested-seq login lets a replay-from-1 mid-session work, use replay; else glimpse with the scenario-d caveat), final state per that mode's invariant.
   For "identical": write one normalize+compare helper (sort, strip counters that legitimately differ like syncs_completed/dups counters/epoch) and REPORT what you excluded. Scenario runs use the HEAD fixture paced so each completes in <60 s; mark the full-sample baseline comparison as a separate slow test if runtime demands (document how to run it).
3. RECOVERY.md — operator runbook: per failure mode (FH crash, DB crash, exchange drop, cold start, both dead): what happens automatically, expected log lines (quote real ones from your runs), what an operator must/must not do, how to verify health afterwards (dbquery STATS fields, mcast_spy).
4. If (and only if) you find an actual bug in jnxfh/jnxdb recovery behavior while testing: fix it minimally in cpp/, keep all existing tests green, and describe the bug+fix prominently in your final message.

BOUNDARY CONDITIONS to cover (from the plan): partial SYNC on disconnect must wipe (already unit-tested — but scenario b implicitly re-exercises it live); kill between DB write and mcast send is acceptable datagram loss (do NOT assert mcast count == db count in kill scenarios; DB is authoritative); stale-DB-from-previous-session detection is covered by the FH's strict mismatch→RESET+resync rule.

VERIFICATION (run all, paste tails):
  python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_recovery.py -q     # all 5 scenarios green
  python3 tools/run_e2e.py --scenario kill_fh --fixture tests/fixtures/sample_udp_head.itch   # prints PASS + result paths
  make -C cpp test 2>&1 | tail -3                              # C++ suite untouched/green
  python3 -m pytest -q                                         # whole suite green

PITFALLS (from the plan — take seriously): trigger on observed message counts, never sleeps; SIGKILL (not SIGTERM) for crash scenarios; never relax a failing state comparison — divergence is a real bug, bisect with smaller fixtures; ensure the partial-sync wipe never triggers on live-path disconnects. Also: clean up ALL child processes even on test failure (pytest fixtures with finalizers), and don't leave orphaned sockets in /tmp.

Final message: files created/changed, per-scenario PASS list with the key evidence (resume log line, sync counters, comparison result), what the normalizer excludes, any bug found+fixed, any simulator limitations you had to design around.

content.text:
F6 is running on a fresh Sonnet agent (Agent C, effectively replacing Agent A whose transcript had grown to ~325k tokens over four tasks). The prompt front-loads everything the fresh context needs: the exact binaries/flags, the simulator CLI, the tools, and the five kill/restart scenarios with their invariants — including the subtleties (progress-based triggers instead of sleeps, SIGKILL only, DB-authoritative so multicast counts aren't asserted in kill windows, and the GLIMPSE trade-history caveat as an explicit expected difference in scenario d).

It delivers `tools/run_e2e.py` (the orchestrator, with a fallback TCP-proxy trick for forcing exchange drops if the simulator lacks scripted disconnects), `tests/integration/test_recovery.py` with all five scenarios compared against an uninterrupted baseline, and a `RECOVERY.md` operator runbook quoting real log lines. Same drill on completion: I re-run the verification, then commit.

## Summary

**Files created:**
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/tools/run_e2e.py` — process orchestrator for all 6 scenarios (`baseline`, `kill_fh`, `kill_db`, `drop_exchange`, `glimpse_cold`, `kill_both`), driven entirely by polling `jnxdb` STATS (`poll_progress`), never bare sleeps except the scenario-mandated "restart N seconds later" delay. Uses the simulator's built-in `--drop-after`/`--glimpse-cut` (no TCP proxy needed — the simulator already supports scripted disconnects). Includes the normalize+compare helper (`normalize_csv`, `compare_dump_dirs`) and an exact post-cut trades reference (`compute_post_cut_trades`, replays the fixture once through the Python prototype `Market` and diffs cumulative stats at the cut boundary — not a naive/wrong isolated-tail replay).
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/tests/integration/test_recovery.py` — 5 pytest scenarios on `sample_udp_head.itch`, paced, each well under 60s.
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/RECOVERY.md` — operator runbook with real quoted log lines per failure mode, `dbquery`/`mcast_spy` health checks, boundary conditions, and the bug writeup.

**Files changed:**
- `/workspace/jnx-feed/cpp/fh/publish.cpp` (`build_sync_dump`) — bug fix, see below.

**Per-scenario PASS evidence:**
- **a. kill_fh**: `recover: recovered from db: 189 orders, ... last_seq=800`, `jnxfh: resume mode: session='SIM0000001' seq=801` — resumed seq exactly `db_last_exch_seq+1`; `dups_dropped=0`; final state byte-identical to baseline (3/3 trials).
- **b. kill_db**: `publish: db write failed (Broken pipe); marking db disconnected` → `jnxfh: pushed RESET + full sync to db (23317 bytes...)` → `jnxfh: db link restored`; `syncs_completed>=1`; mcast `gaps=0` throughout the outage; final state identical.
- **c. drop_exchange**: `soup: connection lost (peer closed); retrying in 1000 ms` → re-login at `seq=801`; `dups_dropped=0`; mcast `pub_seq` gap-free; final state identical.
- **d. glimpse_cold**: orders/books/static `OK` against a full from-seq-1 `book_dump` reference; trades `MISMATCH` there (expected) but `OK` against the exact computed post-cut delta.
- **e. kill_both**: both killed, DB restarted then FH (`--bootstrap=replay`); since this simulator tolerates a fresh from-seq-1 relogin mid-session, final state — **including trade history** — is byte-identical to baseline.

**Normalizer exclusions (documented in `run_e2e.py`):** `epoch`, `syncs_completed`/`syncs_discarded`, `orders_applied`/`ticks_applied`, `dups_dropped` (asserted `==0` separately), `updates_applied` (structurally different after a resync); per-row `last_system_event`/`last_exch_seq`/`last_update_ns` (state) and `last_trade_ns` (trades) — recency stamps a resync legitimately rewrites wholesale; system-wide empty-ticker pseudo-rows — same exclusion `tools/compare_db_dump.py` already makes.

**Bug found and fixed:** `build_sync_dump` in `cpp/fh/publish.cpp` resolved a ticker's `group` from `refdata`, falling back to scanning *live* orders if unknown — but if all that ticker's orders had already closed by resync time, it fell through and emitted a sync row with an **empty group**, creating a duplicate/wrongly-keyed DB row alongside the correctly-grouped row a later live update would create. Fixed by skipping the sync-dump row when the group is genuinely unresolvable (documented residual: such a ticker's row is then simply absent post-resync rather than duplicated-and-wrong, an inherent Market-core information gap shared with the Python prototype, not new to F6). A secondary improvement in the same function re-emits system-wide `(ticker="", group=g)` pseudo-rows from `PubContext.last_sys_event` on resync, which were previously silently dropped. Both fixes are confined to the F5/F6-only resync mechanism; wire format, market core, and DB ingest protocol are untouched. `make -C cpp test` (16/16) and the whole suite (239/239) stay green.

**Simulator limitations designed around:** none required a workaround — `jnxfeed.sim` already exposes `--drop-after` (scripted disconnect) and `--glimpse-cut` (int or fraction), so no custom TCP proxy was needed.

**Verification run:** `pytest tests/integration/test_recovery.py -q` → 5 passed; `run_e2e.py --scenario kill_fh` → PASS; `make -C cpp test` → 16/16; full `pytest -q` → 239 passed. No orphaned processes or sockets after teardown.</result>

content.text:
**F6 is complete, independently verified, and committed (`efe31dd`).** The restart matrix is now proven live, not just designed:

- **All five kill/restart scenarios pass** against the uninterrupted baseline: FH SIGKILL → recovery from jnxdb with resume at exactly `last_seq+1` and zero dup drops; DB SIGKILL → FH keeps multicasting gap-free through the outage, then RESET + full resync; exchange drop → Soup re-login resume, pub_seq contiguous; GLIMPSE cold start → orders/books/static identical with the trades delta matched against an *exactly computed* post-snapshot reference (the agent replayed the fixture through the Python prototype to derive the expected cut-boundary difference, rather than hand-waving it); dual kill → full re-bootstrap, byte-identical including trades.
- **The exercise caught a real bug**: the FH's resync dump could emit a wrongly-keyed row for a ticker whose group was no longer resolvable (all its orders closed by resync time), creating a duplicate DB row. Fixed minimally in `cpp/fh/publish.cpp`; it also now re-emits system-wide event pseudo-rows on resync instead of dropping them. This is exactly the class of defect F6 existed to flush out.
- No simulator workarounds were needed — the prototype's sim already had `--drop-after` and `--glimpse-cut` from Phase 1.
- My independent check: clean rebuild (65 C++ tests), the 5 recovery scenarios (~59 s), the whole 239-test suite, plus a spot re-run of the `kill_db` scenario → PASS. `RECOVERY.md` gives operators the runbook with real log lines.

The fresh-agent approach worked well — F6 completed in one context without needing Agent A's history.

Remaining: **F7** (the Python 3.6 web GUI — multicast receiver, hand-rolled WebSocket server, single-page book/trades view) and **F8** (bench, ASAN/soak hardening, ops docs, RHEL validation kit). F7 is a natural fit for Agent B, whose context already holds the record format and py3.6 discipline. Want me to proceed?

input.description: F7 Python 3.6 web GUI client
input.prompt:
You are implementing task F7 — the jnxweb Python 3.6 web GUI client — for a market-data feed-handler project in the git repo /workspace/jnx-feed. Do NOT run any git commands (the orchestrator commits). Phases F0–F6 are committed and green; do not modify existing files except where stated.

CONTEXT:
- A C++ feed handler (cpp/build/jnxfh) multicasts one UPDATE record per exchange message (except timestamps) to UDP group 239.192.1.1:26400 (dev: loopback iface, IP_MULTICAST_LOOP). Each ~433-byte record carries FULL per-ticker state: static (isin, round_lot, limits...), state (trading_state, short_sell, ref_price...), top-10 aggregated book levels + totals, trade summary (last trade + cum qty/turnover/count), envelope (epoch, pub_seq, exch_seq, exch_ns, trigger char, ticker, group), and an order-level delta section clients ignore. Because every record is full state, a missed datagram self-heals on the next update for that ticker — clients need loss STATS only, no recovery.
- jnxweb/records.py already exists (committed): py3.6-safe decode/encode for all record kinds. USE IT — do not duplicate parsing. Read it first for the exact API and field names. docs/wire_spec.md is the layout contract.
- Existing consumer to imitate for multicast socket setup: tools/mcast_spy.py (group join via IP_ADD_MEMBERSHIP, SO_REUSEADDR etc.).
- Full local pipeline for live testing: 
    cpp/build/jnxdb --sock=/tmp/f7.sock --query_port=27701 &
    python3 -m jnxfeed.sim --itch-file tests/fixtures/sample_udp.itch --itch-port 25831 --glimpse-port 25832 [--speed ...? check --help; use a paced mode for a long-running demo, max speed for quick tests] &
    cpp/build/jnxfh --itch_host=127.0.0.1 --itch_port=25831 --user=TEST --pass=SECRET --db_sock=/tmp/f7.sock --bootstrap=replay --mcast_group=239.192.1.1 --mcast_port=26400 --mcast_if=127.0.0.1 &

HARD ENVIRONMENT RULES (violations = task failure):
- jnxweb/ is PRODUCTION code for Python 3.6 on RHEL 8: stdlib only, and it must BOTH run on the dev interpreter (python3 = 3.14) AND pass `python3 tools/py36check.py <files>`. Read /workspace/jnx-feed/JNX_PLAN.md §0 for the full 3.6 rules: NO asyncio (banned entirely), no dataclasses, no walrus, no f-string`=`, no capture_output/text kwargs, selectors-based single-threaded I/O like the prototype (see jnxfeed/net/reactor.py for the established pattern).
- No pip, no external packages, no CDN assets — the web page must be fully self-contained (inline CSS/JS).

FIRST read: /workspace/jnx-feed/JNX_PLAN2.md Phase F7 (all subsections — implementation steps, boundary conditions, verification, pitfalls) and §1 architecture; jnxweb/records.py; tools/mcast_spy.py; JNX_PLAN.md §0.

DELIVERABLES (per plan F7, restated):
1. jnxweb/mcast.py — non-blocking multicast receiver registered on a shared selectors loop; also a --test-feed injection path: an optional AF_UNIX datagram socket path accepted as an alternative record source (lets tests inject canned records without multicast; document it).
2. jnxweb/state.py — ticker -> latest decoded UPDATE dict; per-ticker recent-trades ring (last 50, appended only when trigger=='E'); global stats (updates, pub_seq gap count, bad datagrams, last epoch); ON EPOCH CHANGE: clear all state, remember event for a UI banner ("feed restarted").
3. jnxweb/httpd.py — hand-rolled HTTP server on the SAME selectors loop (single thread, no http.server, no threading): GET / -> the page; GET /tickers -> JSON sorted ticker list; GET /snap/<ticker> -> JSON full state incl. trades ring (404 JSON if unknown); GET /stats -> JSON global stats; GET /ws -> WebSocket upgrade. Careful HTTP basics: Content-Length, minimal request parsing (method+path+headers), 404/400 paths, connection close handling.
4. jnxweb/wsock.py — minimal RFC 6455 SERVER: handshake Sec-WebSocket-Accept = base64(sha1(key + "258EAFA5-E914-47DA-95CA-C5AB0DC85B11")) — unit-test against the RFC 6455 §1.3 example key "dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBub25jZQ==" -> "s3pPLMBiTxaQ9kYGzzhZRbK+xOo="; server->client TEXT frames UNmasked; parse client frames (which ARE masked): text (subscription JSON {"sub":"TICKER"}), close (reply close), ping (reply pong); fragmentation unsupported (reject/close, documented). Per-client: one subscribed ticker at a time; push that ticker's full JSON on updates, coalesced to max 10 pushes/s per client (latest wins, via loop timer); send-buffer cap 256 KB -> drop client with WARN, never block the loop.
5. jnxweb/static_page.py — one embedded HTML+JS+CSS page: ticker input + clickable list (from /tickers, refreshed periodically), static/state panel, 10-level book table (bid qty/price | price/ask qty, best row on top), last-50 trades table (newest first), stats footer (updates/s, gaps, ws connection state), auto-reconnecting WebSocket, "feed restarted" banner on epoch change. Prices are raw ints with 1 implied decimal — render /10 with one decimal in JS (0x7FFFFFFF = show "-"). Keep it clean and readable; no external fonts/libs; fine to be minimal-pretty (monospace tables ok).
6. jnxweb/__main__.py — args: --mcast 239.192.1.1:26400, --http-port 8080, --mcast-if 127.0.0.1, --test-feed <uds-path> (optional). Wire everything on one selectors loop. INFO log lines per plan format. Clean SIGINT shutdown.
7. tools/ws_probe.py — dev/ops helper (also 3.6-safe): raw-socket WebSocket CLIENT for verification: connects, handshakes (client frames masked per RFC!), sends {"sub": "<ticker>"}, prints N frames (--frames N), exits 0. No external libs.
8. tests/unit/test_jnxweb.py (+ more files if cleaner) — pytest, no live multicast needed: wsock handshake RFC vector; frame encode/decode incl. masked client frames, 16-bit and 64-bit lengths, ping/pong, close; state epoch-clear and trades-ring; httpd routing via a real socketpair or loopback socket against the running loop in-process (spin the selectors loop in the test thread or step it manually — keep it deterministic, no sleeps >0.2s); end-to-end-ish: start jnxweb as a subprocess with --test-feed, inject canned UPDATE records built with jnxweb.records encoders, then assert /tickers, /snap/<ticker>, /stats via urllib.request, and a ws_probe subscription receives frames with correct fields. Collision-safe ports.

BOUNDARY CONDITIONS (from plan): unknown ticker snap -> 404 {"error":"unknown"}; ws sub to unknown ticker -> {"error":"unknown"} then data flows if it appears later; epoch change mid-session -> clear + banner; slow client -> capped + dropped without stalling; multiple concurrent browsers on different tickers; datagram with bad magic/version -> count bad, ignore; pub_seq gaps -> stats only.

VERIFICATION (run all, paste outputs):
  python3 tools/py36check.py jnxweb/*.py tools/ws_probe.py     # ALL clean
  python3 -m pytest tests/unit/test_jnxweb.py -q
  python3 -m pytest -q                                          # whole suite green
  LIVE demo (paste): start the full pipeline (commands above, paced sim), then:
    python3 -m jnxweb --http-port 18080 --mcast-if 127.0.0.1 &
    curl -s localhost:18080/tickers            # non-empty JSON list while replaying
    curl -s localhost:18080/snap/<a-ticker-from-the-list> | head -c 400
    curl -s localhost:18080/stats
    python3 tools/ws_probe.py localhost:18080 <ticker> --frames 5   # 5 live frames printed
  Clean up all processes. Also: after the sim finishes at max speed, /stats gap count on loopback should be small/zero — report it.

PITFALLS (plan F7): asyncio/websockets/aiohttp DO NOT EXIST here — hand-roll on selectors; browser->server frames are masked, server->browser NOT; unbounded JSON buffering to a slow client is the classic bug — cap it; json with sort_keys=True for stable tests; SO_REUSEADDR before bind. Final message: files created, verification outputs incl. live-demo transcript and the gap count, design notes (coalescing, test-feed), any deviations.
